# Mercure.rocks

> Mercure is an open real-time protocol that pushes updates from a server to connected clients over plain HTTP and Server-Sent Events. The Mercure.rocks Hub is the production-ready, AGPL-3.0 reference implementation, with built-in JWT authorization, reconnection, replay, and a typed-matcher subscription model. Use it for LLM token streaming, AI agent state sync, live dashboards, collaborative editing, async job progress, in-app notifications, and any case where a server has fresh data to push to web, mobile, or server clients.

## Core Concepts

- [Introduction to Mercure](https://mercure.rocks/docs/introduction): What Mercure is, what it ships with, and how it differs from WebSockets, Pusher, Ably, WebSub, and Web Push.
- [Topics and Matchers](https://mercure.rocks/docs/concepts/topics-and-matchers): Subscribing with `match`, `matchURLPattern`, `matchRegexp`, `matchCEL`, and `matchURITemplate` query parameters in Mercure 1.0.
- [Subscribing to Mercure Updates](https://mercure.rocks/docs/concepts/subscribing): Open SSE subscriptions from browsers (`EventSource`), Node.js, Go, Python, and other clients.
- [Publishing Real-Time Updates](https://mercure.rocks/docs/concepts/publishing): POST form-encoded updates with topics, alternate topics, `private=on`, custom IDs, and types.
- [Mercure Authorization with JWT](https://mercure.rocks/docs/concepts/authorization): Mint and present JWTs with the object-form `mercure.publish` and `mercure.subscribe` claims, plus cookies and JWKS.
- [Reconnection and History](https://mercure.rocks/docs/concepts/reconnection-and-history): `Last-Event-ID`, `lastEventID=earliest`, history buffer sizing, and detecting data loss.
- [Active Subscriptions and Presence](https://mercure.rocks/docs/concepts/active-subscriptions): Subscription events and the JSON-LD subscription API for presence and live-collab UIs.
- [End-to-End Encryption with JWE](https://mercure.rocks/docs/concepts/encryption): Encrypt update payloads so the Mercure hub itself cannot read them.

## Setup and Deployment

- [Mercure Quickstart](https://mercure.rocks/docs/getting-started/quickstart): Run the hub with Docker, subscribe with `EventSource`, publish with `curl`, in five minutes.
- [Install the Mercure Hub](https://mercure.rocks/docs/getting-started/installation): Docker, Docker Compose, Helm, prebuilt binaries, Arch Linux, custom Caddy builds.
- [Mercure Hub Configuration](https://mercure.rocks/docs/deployment/configuration): Caddyfile directives, environment variables, transports, CORS, JWKS.
- [Run Mercure with Docker](https://mercure.rocks/docs/deployment/docker): Official Docker image, Compose, healthchecks, rootless deployment.
- [Deploy Mercure on Kubernetes](https://mercure.rocks/docs/deployment/kubernetes): Helm chart, SSE-aware probes, rolling updates, rootless security context.
- [Mercure Behind a Reverse Proxy](https://mercure.rocks/docs/deployment/reverse-proxy): NGINX, Traefik, Caddy, HAProxy, AWS ALB, and Cloudflare with SSE-friendly settings.
- [Mercure in GitHub Actions](https://mercure.rocks/docs/deployment/github-actions): Run a Mercure hub as a service container for integration tests.

## AI and LLM Use Cases

- [LLM Token Streaming with Mercure](https://mercure.rocks/docs/use-cases/llm-token-streaming): Stream OpenAI, Anthropic, or local-model tokens to the browser without a WebSocket gateway.
- [AI Agent Progress Streaming](https://mercure.rocks/docs/use-cases/ai-agent-progress): Push tool calls, step transitions, and structured agent state to the UI in real time.

## Application Use Cases

- [Real-Time Dashboards and Live Data](https://mercure.rocks/docs/use-cases/live-data): Stock tickers, IoT telemetry, availability counters, observability dashboards.
- [Collaborative Editing on Mercure](https://mercure.rocks/docs/use-cases/collaborative-editing): Multi-user editing with Yjs, Automerge, or Loro CRDTs.
- [Async Jobs and Background Progress](https://mercure.rocks/docs/use-cases/async-jobs): Notify users when long-running jobs progress or complete.
- [In-App Notifications and Badges](https://mercure.rocks/docs/use-cases/notifications): Per-user notifications, broadcast announcements, multi-tab consistency.
- [Hotwire Turbo Streams over Mercure](https://mercure.rocks/docs/use-cases/hotwire): Push HTML fragments to the browser with Hotwire and Mercure.
- [GraphQL Subscriptions over Mercure](https://mercure.rocks/docs/use-cases/graphql): Back GraphQL subscriptions with Mercure topics and SSE.

## Production

- [Mercure High Availability](https://mercure.rocks/docs/production/high-availability): Self-Hosted Redis, PostgreSQL, Kafka, and Pulsar transports for multi-node Mercure.
- [Rolling Updates and Graceful Shutdown](https://mercure.rocks/docs/production/rolling-updates): Drain SSE connections cleanly during deploys.
- [Health Checks and Monitoring](https://mercure.rocks/docs/production/health-monitoring): Transport-aware probes, Prometheus metrics, alerts, dashboards.
- [Load Testing the Mercure Hub](https://mercure.rocks/docs/production/load-testing): Gatling-based load test for capacity and bottleneck analysis.
- [Debugging the Mercure Hub](https://mercure.rocks/docs/production/debugging): pprof CPU, heap, goroutine, and trace profiles.
- [Mercure Troubleshooting](https://mercure.rocks/docs/production/troubleshooting): 401, 403, CORS, dropped connections, and other common Mercure issues.

## Reference

- [Mercure Protocol Specification](https://mercure.rocks/spec/mercure): The IETF Internet-Draft canonical protocol specification.
- [Mercure Protocol Overview](https://mercure.rocks/docs/reference/protocol): Quick orientation to subscriptions, publications, authorization, replay, and matchers.
- [Mercure FAQ](https://mercure.rocks/docs/reference/faq): Comparisons with WebSockets, Pusher, Ably, WebSub, Web Push, plus connection limits and history.
- [Mercure Hub License](https://mercure.rocks/docs/reference/license): AGPL-3.0 reference hub, open protocol specification, commercial Self-Hosted licensing.
- [Mercure 1.0 Upgrade Guide](https://mercure.rocks/docs/upgrade): Migration from Mercure 0.x to 1.0 — typed matchers, object-form JWT claims, new subscription API.

## Ecosystem

- [Awesome Mercure](https://mercure.rocks/docs/ecosystem/awesome): Libraries, framework integrations, demos, and learning resources.
- [Mercure Conformance Tests](https://mercure.rocks/docs/ecosystem/conformance-tests): Playwright-based test suite to validate any Mercure hub implementation.

## Optional

- [Mercure.rocks Pricing](https://mercure.rocks/pricing): Open-source vs. managed Cloud vs. Self-Hosted Mercure tiers.
- [Mercure GitHub Repository](https://github.com/dunglas/mercure): Source code, releases, and issue tracker for the reference Mercure.rocks Hub.
